image_sources.description: Full side profile view of the Erasmus Bridge from the south, showing the complete pylon and cable-stayed span
image_sources.useful_for: Overall bridge silhouette and proportions - use to establish the total shape and pylon-to-deck ratio in Minecraft

image_sources.description: Erasmus Bridge seen from Euromast observation tower - aerial/elevated side view showing full bridge in context with Rotterdam skyline
image_sources.useful_for: Bird's eye perspective to understand how the bridge sits in the river - helpful for planning Minecraft placement and scale

image_sources.description: Evening/dusk view of the Erasmus Bridge with dramatic lighting
image_sources.useful_for: Night lighting reference - useful if building with glowstone/sea lantern accents to replicate the illuminated look

image_sources.description: Close-up views of the asymmetric pylon ('The Swan') showing the distinctive bent shape and cable attachment points
image_sources.image_url: browse category for multiple pylon close-ups
image_sources.useful_for: Critical reference for building the pylon shape in Minecraft - the asymmetric bend is the hardest part to replicate

image_sources.description: Multiple professional photos of the bridge from various angles including deck level, waterline, and cityscape views
image_sources.image_url: visit page for UNStudio official project photography
image_sources.credit: UNStudio / Christian Richters (typical photographer for UNStudio projects)
image_sources.useful_for: High quality architectural photography showing structural details, cable arrangement, and deck cross-section

image_sources.description: Detailed bridge photos and structural information with multiple angles
image_sources.image_url: visit page for multiple technical bridge photos
image_sources.useful_for: Engineering-focused photos that show structural members and deck construction details

image_sources.description: Architectural photography collection from Archilovers project page
image_sources.image_url: visit page for curated architectural photos
image_sources.useful_for: Professional angles showing the bridge as architecture - good for understanding the design intent

image_sources.description: Blog post with detailed photos and engineering analysis of the Erasmus Bridge
image_sources.image_url: visit page for annotated structural photos
image_sources.useful_for: Annotated photos with structural engineering commentary - helps understand which parts are load-bearing for Minecraft accuracy

image_sources.description: Various stock and editorial photos showing the bridge at night with reflections on the Nieuwe Maas river
image_sources.image_url: browse for night photography reference (watermarked previews)
image_sources.useful_for: Night lighting patterns and reflection reference for optional Minecraft lighting design

image_sources.description: Flickr photo of the bridge showing cable-stayed structure and pylon from waterline perspective
image_sources.image_url: visit page for full resolution photo
image_sources.useful_for: Low-angle perspective useful for understanding the underside of the deck and cable geometry

image_sources.description: Archiweb detailed project page with technical photos and project data
image_sources.image_url: visit page for technical architectural photography
image_sources.useful_for: Technical details and construction photos for understanding how structural elements connect

architectural_drawings.description: WikiArquitectura project page with plans, elevations, and technical drawings of the Erasmo/Erasmus Bridge
architectural_drawings.image_url: visit page - contains elevation drawings, plans, and section drawings inline

architectural_drawings.image_url: archival collection - some images viewable online, full collection at CCA Montreal

architectural_drawings.description: World Architects project page with architectural drawings and project documentation
Archello project page with drawings and technical documentation
architectural_drawings.image_url: visit page for project documentation and drawings

architectural_drawings.description: Architectuul documentation page with architectural information and drawings
Dedicated UNStudio monograph book on the Erasmus Bridge - contains comprehensive sketches, models, renderings, and working drawings
architectural_drawings.image_url: book reference - 'UN Studio/Erasmus Bridge' by Todd Gannon, ISBN 978-1568984261

minecraft_build_notes.scale_suggestion: At 1:10 scale the bridge would be about 80 blocks long with a 14-block tall pylon. At 1:5 scale it would be 160 blocks long with a 28-block pylon.
key_features_to_capture: Asymmetric pylon shape - the 'swan neck' bend is the signature element
Fan-shaped cable stays from pylon top (32 cables) and backstays (8 cables)
The bascule (drawbridge) section at the south end
Pale blue/light gray color of the pylon
The horizontal base section of the pylon before it bends upward

suggested_materials.pylon: Light blue concrete or light gray concrete
suggested_materials.cables: Iron bars, chains, or end rods
suggested_materials.deck: Gray concrete or smooth stone slabs
suggested_materials.night_lighting: Sea lanterns or end rods along the deck edge
